text.en: You are organizing a massive surprise birthday party for your best friend.
I meticulously hide the gifts and keep track of the RSVPs.
When they almost find out early, I stay completely calm and divert their attention.
I throw out the standard party idea and suggest an alien-invasion theme.
I figure out how to fix the broken speaker system just before they arrive.
I make sure everyone has a specific task so we all work together seamlessly.
A fire drill goes off right in the middle of a very important final exam.
I calmly put down my pencil and tell others not to panic.
I actually enjoy the random disruption and treat it like a fun break.
I quickly grab a paperweight to keep our exams from blowing away.
I help the teacher make sure the entire class stays together in line.
I analyze the situation to determine if the test will likely be voided.
You enter a school photography contest with a vague theme.
I take pictures from completely weird, upside-down angles just to be different.
I build a custom tripod rig out of PVC pipes to get the perfect shot.
I share my expensive camera lenses with other students who need them.
I critique the lighting in my photos and adjust the exposure settings logically.
I invent a completely new editing style that makes the photos look like paintings.
Your bike tire pops halfway to school and you are running late.
I temporarily fix the inner tube using some duct tape from my backpack.
I text a friend who drives to school and ask them to pick me up.
I realize I can't make it to first period, so I email my teacher immediately.
I ditch the bike at a shop and use my skateboard for the rest of the way.
I quickly look up a video and learn how to patch a tire in two minutes.
Your history teacher assigns a group project to make an educational TikTok.
I choreograph a dance that gets all group members involved.
I analyze the TikTok algorithm to figure out what hooks will get the most views.
I design a hilarious historical costume out of cardboard and aluminum foil.
I pick up the complex video editing software incredibly fast.
I use an AI voice generator to narrate the script perfectly.
You are designing a custom organizer for your messy school locker.
I analyze the flaws in standard lockers and figure out why they waste space.
I invent a fold-out desk that pops out when the door opens.
I rapidly build and test multiple cardboard prototypes until one works.
I ask an AI to calculate the optimal shelf dimensions based on standard textbooks.
I use a CAD program to 3D print the exact pieces I need.
Your best friend breaks their dominant arm and needs help at school.
I invent a clever shoulder-strap system to carry both our backpacks.
I learn how to write legibly with my non-dominant hand just to show them how.
I set up an AI speech-to-text program on their laptop so they can 'type' essays.
I reconfigure their keyboard settings for one-handed accessibility.
I make sure they are eating calcium-rich foods to help their bone heal faster.
You decide to make an indie film with your friends over the summer.
I learn the basics of cinematography and color grading over a single weekend.
I use AI tools to generate crazy sci-fi backgrounds we can't afford to film.
I manage the camera equipment, microphones, and the massive video files.
I ensure all our action scenes and stunts have proper safety pads.
I spend hours directing the actors to get the perfect emotional performance.
You start a sneaker resale side hustle to make some extra cash.
I use AI models to predict which sneaker drops will skyrocket in value.
I write scripts and use bots to automate the checkout process.
I carefully clean and restore old shoes using special chemicals.
I take highly aesthetic, stylized photos of the shoes for my Instagram page.
I track every purchase, sale, and shipping cost in a detailed spreadsheet.
Your smartphone screen shatters and you decide to fix it yourself.
I follow a teardown video to disconnect the tiny ribbon cables.
I make sure no tiny glass shards cut my fingers during the repair.
I buy a customized, hand-painted replacement back glass just for fun.
I find the cheapest wholesale supplier for screens to save money.
I read the warranty terms carefully to see if opening the phone voids it.
You volunteer to help run a massive city marathon.
I stand at the medical tent handing out water and checking for dehydration.
I paint cool face designs and numbers on the runners before the race.
I manage the merchandise booth, selling t-shirts to the spectators.
I verify that the running route has all the required city permits.
I help construct the massive scaffolding for the finish line.
Your art club takes a field trip to an impressive modern museum.
I sit quietly in front of the statues and try to sketch them accurately.
I go to the gift shop and calculate what I can buy with my ten dollars.
I make sure everyone follows the strict 'no flash photography' rules.
I fix a wobbly display stand that was about to fall over.
I talk to the tour guide, asking them lots of questions about their job.
You are put in charge of the school's student supply store.
I run sales promotions to get rid of the old pencil inventory.
I check the tax regulations on selling snacks on school property.
I build new, sturdy display racks out of spare lumber.
I eagerly greet the freshmen and help them find their classes.
I replace all our plastic products with eco-friendly, recycled alternatives.
You join the school's Model United Nations conference.
I aggressively debate international treaties and international law.
I run the microphone and timer system to keep the speeches on track.
I pass notes and form alliances with other delegates during breaks.
I sponsor a resolution focused entirely on global carbon emissions.
I read hundreds of pages of background guides on the countries.
You decide to build a treehouse in your backyard.
I hammer the nails and construct the wooden frame myself.
I invite all my friends over to hang out in it once it's built.
I make sure to use reclaimed wood so I don't harm new trees.
I calculate the load-bearing capacity of the main branches.
I shout to all the neighbors and throw a massive treehouse party.
You organize a weekend cleanup event at the local state park.
I hand out trash bags and make sure every volunteer feels welcome.
I focus on planting new native flowers near the park entrance.
I track the types of litter we find to report to the city council.
I stand on a bench and give a loud, motivating speech to the crowd.
I put my headphones on and quietly pick up trash deep in the woods alone.
Your science lab group has to dissect a frog.
I make sure all biological waste is disposed of in the eco-friendly bin.
I read the textbook manual ahead of time so I know the anatomy.
I take charge and enthusiastically lead the dissection for the squeamish kids.
I prefer to do the cutting quietly and carefully without making jokes.
I meticulously clean and organize the scalpels and trays afterward.
You are writing a massive 20-page research paper for history class.
I dive into primary source documents and ancient books to find evidence.
I do a highly animated oral presentation of my findings to the class.
I lock myself in my room for three days to write in absolute silence.
I format every single citation flawlessly according to the rubric.
When I get a bad grade on the draft, I just sigh and start rewriting calmly.
You go to a massive, incredibly crowded mall on Black Friday.
I thrive in the chaos and loudly bargain with the kiosk vendors.
I retreat to the quietest corner of the bookstore to escape the noise.
I bring a map and strike off every store we need to visit in order.
I don't get annoyed by the long lines; I just patiently wait my turn.
I decide to try a completely strange new food from the food court.
You are playing a notoriously difficult, frustrating video game.
I turn off the multiplayer chat so I can focus on the single-player mode.
I carefully manage my inventory and organize my potions by color.
When I lose to the boss 50 times, I don't rage-quit, I just try again.
I equip completely random, bizarre armor just to see what happens.
I memorize the boss's attack patterns and calculate the exact dodge timing.
You are putting together a 5,000 piece model kit with friends.
I sort all the tiny plastic pieces into numbered bags before we start.
When someone accidentally steps on a piece, I tell them it's not a big deal.
I suggest we build the spaceship backward just for a challenge.
I figure out how to substitute a lost gear with a spare part from another set.
I pass pieces to the builder and read the instructions out loud.
A massive thunderstorm knocks out your home's internet for the night.
I relax and enjoy the break from social media without complaining.
I dig out a strange, dusty board game my family has never played before.
I reset the router and check the IP settings to see if it's a local issue.
I gather my siblings in the living room for a game of charades.
I check the cellular outage map to determine if the whole grid is down.
You are making a custom Halloween costume for a huge party.
I come up with a highly abstract, conceptual costume idea.
I figure out how to wire battery-powered LEDs into the fabric safely.
I coordinate with three friends so we can go as a matching group.
I analyze the weather forecast to decide if the costume needs a coat.
I sculpt a completely original monster mask out of foam and clay.
You completely forget your lunch at home and have no money.
I figure out a clever way to trade a pencil for an apple.
I tell my friends, knowing they will all pitch in and share their snacks.
I realize missing one meal won't hurt me and just drink water instead.
I make a bizarre 'soup' out of free ketchup packets and crackers.
I learn the cafeteria's policy on free emergency lunch vouchers.
Your class is planning a harmless but legendary senior prank.
I coordinate the group chat so nobody accidentally leaks the plan.
I analyze the school's camera blind spots to find the safest route.
I come up with the hilarious idea to cover the hallways in sticky notes.
I quickly learn how to pick the cheap padlock on the gym doors.
I use an AI voice tool to leave a fake voicemail from the principal.
You are upgrading an old PC to make it run games faster.
I benchmark the system to see if the CPU or GPU is the bottleneck.
I design a really cool custom water-cooling loop with neon lights.
I read the motherboard manual and figure out the front panel pins fast.
I use an AI utility program to safely auto-overclock the processor.
I carefully apply the thermal paste and slot the new RAM sticks.
A friend has a sudden panic attack in the middle of the hallway.
I invent a silly distraction game to help ground them in reality.
I rapidly recall the grounding techniques I read about in health class.
I load up an AI-guided breathing exercise on my phone for them to follow.
I text the school counselor on their secure messaging portal.
I get them a cup of water and monitor their heart rate.
You and your friends decide to start a rock band in a garage.
I learn how to play the bass guitar from scratch in two weeks.
I generate cool synth backing tracks using an AI music generator.
I set up the amplifiers, pedals, and PA system so we don't blow a fuse.
I make everyone wear earplugs so we don't get hearing damage.
I draw the edgy skull artwork for our band's first album cover.
You run a highly popular meme page for your high school.
I use an AI bot to generate hilarious captions based on school news.
I write a script that automatically posts at the highest traffic times.
I post memes about getting enough sleep and staying hydrated.
I hand-draw funny caricatures of the teachers to use as templates.
I sell sponsored shoutouts to local pizza places to make money.
You buy a cheap smart-home hub to control your bedroom lights.
I splice the power wires and solder the LED strips myself.
I set the lights to a warm tone that helps reduce eye strain at night.
I program the lights to sync perfectly with the colors in my favorite movies.
I track the wattage to see how much money the LEDs save on the electric bill.
I read the privacy policy to make sure the microphone isn't recording me.
You are the manager for the school's basketball team.
I tape up the players' ankles and keep the ice baths ready.
I design the cool warmup shirts that the players wear.
I organize the concession stand to maximize profits for new uniforms.
I check the league rulebook to ensure all players are academically eligible.
I fix the broken digital scoreboard buzzer with a soldering iron.
Your class gets permission to renovate the dusty senior lounge.
I pick out the paint swatches and design a relaxing color scheme.
I negotiate discounts with local furniture stores to stay under budget.
I check the school fire codes to ensure our couches are compliant.
I assemble the heavy coffee tables and mount the TV to the wall.
I ask everyone for input to ensure the room feels welcoming to all cliques.
You host a weekend car wash to raise money for the band.
I hold the cash box and quickly calculate change for the drivers.
I make sure our signs don't violate the city's advertising ordinances.
I fix the leaky power washer pump using a spare rubber gasket.
I joke around with the drivers and keep the volunteers energized.
I ensure we only use biodegradable soap that won't harm the drains.
You join the school's highly competitive debate club.
I practice arguing both sides of a complex legal case.
I help build the wooden podiums we use during our home tournaments.
I comfort the younger members who are nervous before their speeches.
I focus my arguments entirely on renewable energy legislation.
I dig through library archives to find statistics that crush the opponent.
You are helping a close friend move into a new house.
I take apart the bunk beds and reassemble them in the new room perfectly.
I keep everyone laughing and pass out drinks to keep morale high.
I make sure all the cardboard boxes are broken down and recycled properly.
I study the floor plans to figure out the best way to pivot the couch.
I blast music and turn the unpacking process into a massive dance party.
You get a summer job as a counselor at a sleepaway camp.
I sit with the homesick kids and listen to their problems patiently.
I lead the nature walks and teach the kids not to step on the saplings.
I read books on child psychology to handle camper conflicts better.
I stand on the dining hall tables and lead the loudest camp chants.
I sweep the cabins by myself while the others are at the noisy bonfire.
Your school allows you to build a community garden on an empty lot.
I select native plants that require less water to help the local ecosystem.
I test the soil's pH levels and research which vegetables will grow best.
I enthusiastically give garden tours to the elementary school kids.
I spend hours quietly pulling weeds by myself on Sunday mornings.
I create a strict daily watering schedule and check it off religiously.
You are preparing for the hardest standardized test of the year.
I dig up ten years of past exams to analyze the question patterns.
I host a huge study group where I hype everyone up before the test.
I put my phone on airplane mode and study completely alone in a quiet room.
I use a stopwatch to time my practice sections down to the second.
When I see a question I don't know, I don't panic; I just guess and move on.
Your family goes on a huge, crowded cruise ship for vacation.
I sign up for the karaoke contest and sing in front of hundreds of strangers.
I find a secluded deck chair and read my book away from the pool noise.
I pack my suitcase perfectly so all my outfits are organized by day.
When the ship hits rough waters, I stay totally relaxed and ignore it.
I ignore the burgers and head straight for the weirdest exotic food buffet.
You decide to completely reorganize your incredibly messy closet.
I lock my door so nobody bothers me while I sort through my stuff.
I fold every single shirt perfectly and arrange them by color.
When the clothes pile falls over, I don't get mad, I just restart.
I keep some really weird, outdated clothes just because they look cool.
I figure out a clever way to fit two rows of shoes in a tiny space.
You and your friends are planning a trip to a nearby city.
I make a detailed itinerary with exact train departure times.
When we miss the bus, I calm everyone down and say we'll catch the next one.
I convince everyone to visit a bizarre, underground modern art exhibit.
I figure out how to navigate the complicated subway map to save money.
I use an app to easily split the hotel and food costs evenly among us.
You get lost while walking around a busy downtown area.
I don't freak out; I just enjoy the scenery until I figure it out.
I decide to just hop on a random bus and see where it takes me.
I deduce which way is north based on the sun and the street numbers.
I flag down a group of locals and ask them nicely for directions.
I analyze the city grid map on the bus stop to find the quickest route back.
You enter a school baking competition with a strict time limit.
I invent a crazy jalapeño-chocolate cupcake flavor to stand out.
When my cake burns, I scrape the edges and turn it into cake pops.
I partner with a friend so one mixes while the other handles the oven.
I calculate the exact baking times to ensure I don't run out of time.
I sculpt an incredibly detailed, artistic fondant dragon for the cake.
Your phone dies right before the concert starts and you lose your friends.
I find a security guard and logically explain where I saw them last.
I borrow a stranger's phone to quickly log into my social media and DM them.
I realize finding them in the dark is unlikely and just head to our agreed spot.
I buy a glowing marker and write my friend's name huge on my shirt.
I recall their phone number from memory, even though I usually rely on contacts.
You are creating a comprehensive study guide for midterms.
I share a Google Doc and assign different chapters to my classmates.
I analyze the syllabus to predict exactly which topics the teacher will test.
I draw a massive, colorful mind map to connect all the historical events.
I learn complex markdown formatting to make the document easily searchable.
I use an AI summarizer to condense a 50-page chapter into bullet points.
You find a broken remote-control car at a garage sale.
I reverse-engineer the steering mechanism to find the jammed gear.
I strip the plastic shell and paint it to look like a post-apocalyptic buggy.
I watch a tutorial and successfully solder the tiny broken wires back together.
I ask an AI chatbot for troubleshooting steps for this specific RC model.
I swap out the broken motor with a stronger one from an old drill.
You are brainstorming ideas for a new student fitness app.
I design a weird feature that makes users run away from virtual zombies.
I quickly learn how to use a drag-and-drop app builder to make a prototype.
I integrate an AI that generates a custom workout plan based on user goals.
I code the GPS tracking API so the app accurately measures running distance.
I ensure the app includes warm-up stretches to prevent muscle injuries.
Your friend asks you to direct a music video for their song.
I quickly master the drone camera controls after just a few practice flights.
I use an AI tool to lip-sync and auto-cut the video to the beat.
I adjust the camera's ISO and shutter speed to shoot in low light.
I make sure everyone stays hydrated and takes breaks during the long shoot.
I sketch out a detailed storyboard visualizing the exact cinematic shots.
You decide to start a neighborhood dog walking business.
I use an AI assistant to auto-reply to clients and manage my calendar.
I set up a secure payment portal and GPS tracking app for the owners.
I learn how to safely administer tick medication and handle dog injuries.
I draw a cute, recognizable logo of a paw print for my business cards.
I analyze the local market to set competitive, profitable walking rates.
You accidentally crash your expensive camera drone into a tree.
I re-solder the broken circuit board and recalibrate the gyroscopes.
I wear safety goggles and heavy gloves when handling the shattered propellers.
I take the opportunity to paint the drone a cool neon color before reassembling.
I calculate if it's cheaper to order replacement parts or just buy a used drone.
I check the local aviation rules to ensure I'm not flying it in restricted airspace.
